summary: SQL-themed package is a thin loader for an unrelated third-party dependency
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.0.0 advertises itself as a SQL access library (scope name and keywords node/sql) but its main entry (index.js) only exports trivial add/subtract math functions and unconditionally requires an unrelated third-party package '@antoncarlos1/nodelamp' at module load. The required dependency is from a different scope/author and has no relationship to the advertised SQL functionality, so any installer doing `require('@sql-access/nodesql')` will execute the top-level code of @antoncarlos1/nodelamp without that being apparent from this package's name or documentation. This package itself ships no install-time lifecycle hooks and no payload; the installer-side risk lives entirely in the transitive dependency, which must be evaluated separately. The cover-story shape (purpose mismatch, unrelated scope, empty description, trivial exports masking a foreign require) is consistent with a namespace-abuse loader, but without confirmation that @antoncarlos1/nodelamp is itself malicious, this routes to human review rather than an automatic block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
